message:
perf(packs): bound regex backtracking + deadline-aware safe matching

src/packs/regex_engine.rs:
- Adds a `pub const BACKTRACK_LIMIT: usize = 100_000` and switches the
  three places that build a `fancy_regex::Regex` (`CompiledRegex::new`,
  the `new` fallback path that retries with the backtracking engine
  after the linear engine rejects a pattern, and `new_backtracking`)
  to use `RegexBuilder::new(pattern).backtrack_limit(LIMIT).build()`.
  Adds a sibling `new_with_backtrack_limit(pattern, limit)` so callers
  that want a different bound can pass one through.
- Rationale: `fancy_regex` defaults to 1,000,000 backtracking steps. At
  ~10 ns per step that is ~10 ms of CPU on a single pathological pattern,
  far exceeding the existing 1 ms `PATTERN_MATCH` panic budget. Lowering
  the limit to 100K bounds worst-case evaluation time to ~1 ms; when the
  limit is hit `fancy_regex` returns `BacktrackLimitExceeded`, which our
  match wrappers already treat as "no match" (fail-open). External pack
  authors get the same protection automatically.

src/packs/mod.rs:
- Adds `Pack::matches_safe_with_deadline(cmd, deadline)`: a deadline-aware
  variant of `matches_safe` that:
    * still benefits from the `safe_regex_set` short-circuit (and the
      `safe_regex_set_is_complete` fast-negative when applicable);
    * polls `Deadline::is_exceeded` between individual safe pattern
      probes and bails out (returns `false`, i.e. no safe match) the
      moment the deadline fires.
  This lets callers that already enforce a deadline on the destructive-
  side scan apply the same deadline to the safe-side scan instead of
  having one scan cancel and the other run unbounded.
- Adds `EnabledKeywordIndex::has_any_keyword(...)` documentation hook
  noting the AC-automaton fast path used to skip pack evaluation entirely
  when no enabled keyword appears in the command.

The crate root (`src/lib.rs`) already re-exports `BACKTRACK_LIMIT` so
external integrations can read the bound without depending on the
internal module path.
